name: cli
context: Use the dojo CLI to search, inspect, validate, scaffold, and publish nodes from the terminal
info: The dojo CLI is the primary developer interface for working with the Dojo knowledge graph from the terminal. It wraps the most common workflows into single commands: searching for nodes, inspecting node manifests, validating node.json files, scaffolding new nodes from templates, and publishing to the registry. The CLI can operate against a local nodes/ directory (offline mode) or a running Dojo server (online mode). It auto-detects the dojo repository root and defaults to local mode. Install globally via `npm install -g @dojo/cli` or run with `npx @dojo/cli`. See [[dojo/server]] for the API server the CLI talks to in online mode.
triggers: validate a node from the command line
scaffold a node with the cli
body:
# Dojo CLI

The CLI is the fastest way to interact with Dojo from the terminal. It handles search, inspect, validate, scaffold, and publish — the five operations agents and developers use most.

## Installation

```bash
# Global install
npm install -g @dojo/cli

# Or use npx (no install)
npx @dojo/cli search "deploy to vercel"
```

## Commands

### Search

Find nodes by query, tag, or type:

```bash
# Text search
dojo search "deploy to vercel"

# Filter by tag
dojo search "deploy" --tag vercel

# Filter by type
dojo search "auth" --type context

# Limit results
dojo search "kubernetes" --limit 5
```

### Inspect

View a node's full manifest or specific fields:

```bash
# Full manifest
dojo inspect github/repos

# Specific field
dojo inspect github/repos --field context
dojo inspect github/repos --field scripts
dojo inspect github/repos --field links

# JSON output (for piping)
dojo inspect github/repos --json
```

### Validate

Check a node against the schema and knowledge quality rules:

```bash
# Validate a single node
dojo validate nodes/github/repos/node.json

# Validate an entire subtree
dojo validate nodes/github/

# Schema only
dojo validate nodes/github/repos/node.json --schema-only

# Knowledge quality only
dojo validate nodes/github/repos/node.json --knowledge-only
```

See [[dojo/validate]] for what gets checked.

### Scaffold

Create a new node from a template:

```bash
# Scaffold an ecosystem
dojo scaffold ecosystem redis

# Scaffold a skill under an existing parent
dojo scaffold skill github/actions/workflows

# Scaffold a context node
dojo scaffold context kubernetes/troubleshooting
```

See [[dojo/authoring/scaffold]] for details.

### Publish

Publish a validated node to the registry:

```bash
# Publish to local (smoke test)
dojo publish nodes/github/repos/ --local

# Publish to registry
dojo publish nodes/github/repos/ --registry https://registry.dojo.dev
```

See [[dojo/publish]] for the full workflow.

### Learn

Read a node's knowledge content in the terminal:

```bash
# Learn from a node (renders body + sections)
dojo learn github/repos

# Learn a specific section
dojo learn github/repos#create
```

## Modes

| Mode | When | How |
|------|------|-----|
| **Local** | No server running | Reads directly from `nodes/` directory |
| **Online** | Server running | Queries `DOJO_URL` API endpoints |

The CLI auto-detects mode: if `DOJO_URL` is set and reachable, it uses online mode. Otherwise, it finds the nearest `nodes/` directory and operates locally.

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `DOJO_URL` | _(none)_ | Server URL for online mode |
| `DOJO_ROOT` | _(auto)_ | Path to dojo repository root |
| `DOJO_AUTH_TOKEN` | _(none)_ | Auth token for publish commands |

sections.title: CLI commands
sections.body: Six commands: `search` (find nodes by query/tag/type), `inspect` (view manifest fields), `validate` (check schema + knowledge quality), `scaffold` (create from template), `publish` (push to registry), `learn` (read knowledge content). Each command supports `--help` for usage details and `--json` for machine-readable output.

sections.title: Local vs online mode
sections.body: The CLI operates in two modes. Local mode reads directly from the `nodes/` directory on disk — no server needed. Online mode queries a running Dojo server via `DOJO_URL`. Auto-detection: if `DOJO_URL` is set and the server responds to a health check, use online mode. Otherwise, walk up the directory tree to find the nearest `nodes/` directory and use local mode.

sections.title: Installation
sections.body: Install globally with `npm install -g @dojo/cli` for the `dojo` command. Or use `npx @dojo/cli` for one-off usage without installation. Requires Node.js >= 18. The CLI has no native dependencies — it's pure JavaScript.
